rollout.reason: This slice records marketplace evidence in Git and does not create runtime plugin marketplace state.

evidence: Internal plugin foundations must prove packaging and install boundaries first.
candidates.launchGate: Submission checklist approved by platform, security, and support owners.
candidates.rollback: Do not expose public submission entry points.

evidence: Review must cover package signing, permission boundaries, vulnerability response, and tenant isolation.
candidates.launchGate: Security review checklist is approved and linked from a child spec.
candidates.rollback: Block plugin publishing until manual review passes.

evidence: Publishing depends on submission, review, support, and commercial policy.
candidates.launchGate: Publishing child spec names moderation and rollback controls.
candidates.rollback: Hide marketplace catalog and keep internal plugin registry only.

evidence: SDK versioning must describe supported host APIs and deprecation windows.
candidates.launchGate: SDK compatibility matrix is reviewed before public examples ship.
candidates.rollback: Freeze public SDK claims and keep examples internal.

evidence: Marketplace pricing and revenue share require commercial and legal review.
candidates.launchGate: Commercial owner approves terms and support cost model.

evidence: Support must cover escalation, ownership, SLAs, and partner contact routing.
candidates.rollback: Disable partner listing and route support to internal plugins only.

evidence: Takedown policy must cover abuse, vulnerabilities, tenant impact, and customer notice.
candidates.launchGate: Takedown workflow has security and support approval.
candidates.rollback: Remove marketplace listing and block installs for affected plugin key.
